title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Data Contract Brief: Isolation Anomalies
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql isolation anomalies increment in a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.instruction:
theory case — Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.
